name: SpaceX

overview.value: Space Exploration Technologies Corp., known as SpaceX, is an American aerospace manufacturer and space transport services company headquartered in Hawthorne, California. It was founded in 2002 by entrepreneur Elon Musk with the goal of reducing space transportation costs and enabling the colonization of Mars. SpaceX operates from many pads, on the East Coast of the US they operate from SLC-40 at Cape Canaveral Space Force Station and historic LC-39A at Kennedy Space Center. They also operate from SLC-4E at Vandenberg Space Force Base, California, usually for polar launches. Another launch site is being developed at Boca Chica, Texas.

notes: Seeded with the Orbits facilities layer (2026-07-05); company pages render client-side and state little in plain HTML, so most fields await sourced fill by the weekly maintenance run.

events.headline: SpaceX Falcon 1 reaches orbit on fourth flight, first successful launch
SpaceX launches Dragon on NASA demonstration mission to attempt first ISS berth
SpaceX launches first contracted cargo resupply mission to space station
SpaceX lands Falcon 9 first stage, first successful landing of an orbital rocket booster
SpaceX reflies a Falcon 9 booster with SES-10, first reflight of an orbital class rocket
SpaceX launches Falcon Heavy on its first flight from Kennedy Space Center
SpaceX launches Crew Dragon Demo-2, first crewed flight to station
SpaceX wins NASA Human Landing System contract for Artemis moon lander
FAA grants SpaceX launch license for Starship first integrated flight test
SpaceX launches Starship on first flight test, vehicle lost (per Spaceflight Now)
